events.description: Enjoy a dynamic art program designed to help kids explore their inner creativity, from painting to collage. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-931-5512 or lefrakp@mdpls.org. Ages 6 - 11 yrs.

events.title: Family Storytime
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and activities for children and their families. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-442-7872 or castrod@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: Soul of the Caribbean Show
events.description: Celebrate Haitian Heritage Month Sit back, relax and go on a musical journey with Reggie Paul, one of the Caribbean's most popular performers. For more information, contact the branch at 305-787-1544 or pollasr@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: STEAM Spot
events.description: Presented by SparkstoFlames Education Services Drop in and have fun exploring new STEAM topics with guided, hands-on activities about science, technology, engineering, art, and math. Materials will be provided. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-694-2707 or powellk@mdpls.com. All ages.

events.title: Tech Time
events.description: Need help setting up your tech? Receive one-on-one assistance installing programs, setting up apps, and learning how to use your devices. Bring your own laptop, tablet, or phone - or use one of ours - and together, we'll explore the latest tech trends together. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-751-8689 or millert@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: The Imitation Game (PG-13)
events.description: Jewish American Heritage Month Join us for a screening of The Imitation Game, Biopic of Alan Turing, the brilliant mathematician and computer scientist who helped the Allies secure victory in World War II by cracking the German Enigma code, and who was later prosecuted as a homosexual by his own government. 114 minutes. For more information, contact the bran

events.title: Virtual Homework Help and Tutoring
events.description: The Homework Help & Tutoring Program has gone virtual! Certified teachers host 30-minute online tutoring sessions in reading and/or math on Thursdays from 5 p.m. to 8 p.m. for students in grades K -12. events.title: Galactic Nights: An Evening Under the Stars
events.description: Join us for a stellar night of stargazing with local astrophotographer Dan Becker as he guides us on an exploration of the night sky. Discover the wonders of the cosmos while enjoying stories, crafts and celestial sights. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-553-1134 or gonzalezja@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: Goodnight, Library: Storytime Under the Stars
events.description: Grab your favorite stuffy, put on those jammies, and help us say goodnight to Westchester Library! Join us for stories, sing-alongs, and fun under the stars (weather permitting). For more information, please contact the branch at 305-553-1134 or gonzalezja@mdpls.org. Ages 3 yrs.+.

events.title: Baby Tummy Time
events.description: Parents and caregivers are invited to explore a variety of toys and activities designed to strengthen fine motor skills and encourage healthy growth. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-207-1344 or meyerm@mdpls.org. Ages 0-2 yrs.

events.title: Fun with LEGO®
events.description: Get creative and have fun building with LEGO® blocks. For more information, please contact the library at 305-242-2290 or gonzalezmi@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: Fun with LEGO®
events.description: Bring your creativity and have fun building with LEGO® blocks! For more information, please contact the branch at 305-805-3811 or at mayerr@mdpls.org. Ages 6 - 11 yrs.

events.title: Furry Tales
events.description: Read your favorite stories alongside a friendly dog! Reading aloud to a registered therapy dog may help improve literacy skills and boost confidence. Bring your own book or choose from the library's collection. Presented by the Alliance of Therapy Dogs. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-931-5512 or lefrakp@mdpls.org. Ages 0 - 12 yrs.

events.title: STEM Saturday with Engineers on Wheels
events.description: Presented by FIU's College of Engineering Join us for wheel-y fun STEM challenges at the library! Participants will get an opportunity to learn from current and future engineers through a variety of family-friendly activities focused on engineering, coding, and robotics. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-553-1134 or gonzalezja@mdpls.org.

events.title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
events.description: Presented by Fantasy Factory Theatre Follow the Yellow Brick Road like you’ve never seen it before in Fantasy Theatre Factory’s “The Wonderful Wizard of Oz”—a fast, funny and wildly theatrical spin on L. Frank Baum’s classic, bursting with South Florida flavor, playful surprises and big-hearted adventure. For more information, please contact the branch at 30

events.title: Touch the Bay
events.description: Presented by Miami EcoAdventures Sea stars, conch and sea urchins - oh my! Enjoy an informative presentation on sea grass ecosystems and an interactive touch tank activity featuring some of the amazing critters found in Miami's Biscayne Bay. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-250-4688 or zunigad@mdpls.org. Ages 6 yrs.+

events.title: Wildlife Show
events.description: Summer Reading Challenge Join us for the ultimate animal encounter! Meet a variety of friendly critters during this presentation led by an experienced animal trainer. For more information, please contact the branch at 786‑582-2620 or baileyc@mdpls.org.org. All ages.

events.title: Furry Tales
events.description: Reading with Gator Read your favorite story to a certified therapy dog! Reading aloud to a therapy dog can help improve literacy skills and boost confidence. You may bring your own book or choose one from the library collection. Presented by Community Paws. For more information or to register, please contact the branch at 305-388-0326 or jacobsonf@mdpls.org.

events.title: Books with Blue the Awesome Opossum
events.description: Presented by Pelican Harbor Seabird Station Join us for a visit from the Pelican Harbor Seabird Station! Meet and greet two ambassador animals native to Florida, plus enjoy a book reading and a fun hands-on activity. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-667-6121 or sandar@mdpls.org. Ages 4 yrs.+

events.title: Coconut Grove and its Origins: Ralph Munroe and the Library
events.description: Step into the early days of Coconut Grove and uncover the legacy of Ralph Munroe, one of its most influential pioneers. Explore the story behind The Barnacle and the land he dedicated to institutions that still shape the community today, including the founding of the library. Presented by Vincent Nastasi of The Barnacle Historic State Park. For more informat

events.title: LEGO® Hour
events.description: Join us for open LEGO® building time and let your imagination soar! Building blocks will be provided. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-207-1344 or meyerm@mdpls.org. Ages 0 - 12 yrs.

events.title: Rook & Roll Chess Club
events.description: Sharpen your skills by playing other fans of this classic game. Players of all skill levels and abilities are welcome. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-279-0520 or corredorj@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: STEAM Crafts
events.description: Summer Series Want to try your hand at STEAM based crafts? Participate in our STEAM hour, where we will combine elements of science, technology, engineering, art, and math to learn new things. Materials will be provided. Registration Required. For more information, please contact the library at 305-594-2514 or symonsj@mdpls.org. Ages 6 - 12 yrs. June 27 - Mi

events.title: LEGO® Club
events.description: Participate in a LEGO® STEM challenge and let your imagination run wild. We supply the blocks and you supply the creativity! For more information, please contact the branch at 305-820-8564 or padronc@mdpls.org. Ages 3-12 yrs.

events.title: "Water" You Painting?
events.description: Experiment with watercolor painting and share tips with others during this relaxed painting session suitable for all skill levels! Basic materials will be provided. You are welcome to bring your own materials. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-667-6121 or sandar@mdpls.org. Ages 6 yrs.+

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Sharpen your skills and challenge yourself by playing this classic game. Beginners welcome. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-625-6424 or noelp@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: LEGO®+ BuildWorks Hub
events.description: Discover a space just for you where you can imagine, learn, explore and create! It's all about hands-on fun that encourages innovation, exploration and creativity. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-820-8520 or ruizo@mdpls.org. Ages 8-12 yrs.

events.title: Mark Twain's Jumping Frog of Calaveras County
events.description: Presented by Momentum Dance Company - America 250 Beloved American author Mark Twain’s hilarious classic short story comes to life through dance. Smiley, the gambler, bets that his frog can out-jump any frog in Calaveras County; but the mysterious stranger has something up his sleeve! The story is told through Mark Twain’s prose, bluegrass and country music,

events.title: Minecraft Fun, Skillfully Done!
events.description: Calling all Minecrafters! Sharpen your real-world skills with block-tacular challenges! You've loved building in the game, now bring your creativity to the library for 3D origami block folding, pixel art challenges, art sheets and puzzles and more! Plus, get your favorite Minecraft books displayed. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-820-8

events.title: Screen-free Saturday
events.description: Bring the whole family and unplug with us! Enjoy quality time together without screens, featuring new activities each week to keep things fresh and exciting. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-442-8706 or capleybr@mdpls.org. All ages.

events.title: Switch Saturdays
events.description: Dust off your video game talents in a friendly round of gaming on the Nintendo Switch™. Battle your friends and family and emerge victorious, or simply practice your skills. For more information, please contact the branch at (305) 442-8710 or muniasm@mdpls.org. Ages 6-18 yrs.

events.title: Video Game Club
events.description: Test Your Skills on the Switch! Play and test your Mario Kart skills on the Nintendo Switch™. Whether your goal is becoming a great player or you just want to play and learn, we invite you to join us! All skill levels welcome. For more information, please contact the branch at 305-643-8574 or barreroc@mdpls.org. Ages 6 yrs.+

events.title: Family Movie Day
